zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch於1328年出生在蘇格蘭Linlithgowshire阿爾可時，她的父親Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar35歲，母親Isabella Stewart20歲。她於大約1344年與Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1393年10月19日在英國薩默塞特卡素 加里享年65歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch于1328年出生在苏格兰Linlithgowshire阿爾可时，她的父亲Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar35岁，母亲Isabella Stewart20岁。她于大约1344年与Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1393年10月19日在英国薩默塞特卡素 加里享年65岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Mar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

variants.name: Martin
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch was born in 1328, in Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland, her father, Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar, was 35 and her mother, Isabella Stewart, was 20. She married Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar about 1344, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 19 October 1393, in Castle Cary, Somerset, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch est née en 1328, à Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Écosse, son père, Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Isabella Stewart, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar environ 1344, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 19 octobre 1393, à Castle Cary, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch 1328 in Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar 35 und ihre Mutter Isabella Stewart 20. Sie hat um 1344 in Scotland Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 19. Oktober 1393 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Castle Cary, Somerset, England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch è nata il nel 1328 nel Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland, sua padre, Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Isabella Stewart, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar circa 1344, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 19 ottobre 1393, nel Castle Cary, Somerset, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Mar of Mar and Gariochが1328年にスコットランドLinlithgowshireアバーコルンで生まれた時，父Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Marは35歳，母Isabella Stewartは20歳であった。約1344年にスコットランドでSir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Marと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1393年10月19日にイギリスソメルセトカストル カーイで65歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch(이)가 1328년 스컷랜드 Linlithgowshire 압르코른에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Stewart(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1344년 스컷랜드에서 Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1393년 10월 19일 영국 서머세트 카스틀 카리에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch nasceu em 1328, em Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland, seu pai, Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Stewart, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar aproximadamente 1344, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 19 de outubro de 1393, em Castle Cary, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch родилась в 1328 г. в местности абиркорн, Linlithgowshire, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Stewart, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar около 1344 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 19 октября 1393 г. в местности кастл кэри, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Mar of Mar and Garioch nació en 1328, en Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland, su papá, Donald MacGylocher 8th Earl of Mar, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Isabella Stewart, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar aproximadamente 1344, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 19 de octubre de 1393, en Castle Cary, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Inglaterra.
